*�����������������&The ThreadsMonitor is a small component to be used as a UI top the ThreadManager if in need of a live debugging tool. 
		It's useful to track what a TDThread is doing or how busy the ThreadManager currently is.

		It's also listing all the TDTasks, what's in queue or currently being processed.

*����������������� ># Text Component callbacks

def onValueChange(comp, value, prevValue):
	"""
	Called whenever the text component's value parameter changes for any reason including typing
	in the text window or changing the parameter directly. The callback is equivalent to the
	onValueChange in the parameter exectute DAT.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		value: the new value
		prevValue: the previous value
	"""
	#debug('\nonValueChange comp:', comp.path, '- new value: ', value, ', prev value: ', prevValue )
	return
	
def onFocus(comp):
	"""
	Called when one of the text components viewers gets keyboard focus. 
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
	"""
	#debug('\nonFocus comp:', comp.path)
	return

def onFocusEnd(comp, info):
	"""
	Called when one of the text component's viewers loses keyboard focus. In regular editing mode
	this is right after the contents are saved back to the value parameter. This function is called
	everytime the viewer loses focus, even if the text does not change.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		info: a dictionary containing information about the event including:
			reason: a string indicating what caused the viewer to lose focus e.g. 'enter', 'escape', 'tab', 'unknown'
	
	"""
	#debug('\nonFocusEnd comp:', comp.path, '- info:\n', info)
	return
	
def onTextEdit(comp):
	"""
	Called each time the contents of the text component viewer change i.e. when typing a character,
	deleting text, cutting or pasting. 
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
	"""
	#debug('\nonTextEdit comp:', comp.path, '- text: ', comp.editText)
	return
	
def onTextEditEnd(comp, value, prevValue):
	"""
	Called when the user finishes editing and the value has been changed. Not called in 'Continuous Edit' mode. Use
	onTextEdit and onFocusEnd when using continuous editing.

    Note: Any Python undo blocks created by this callback will be included in the undo step for the change to the text field.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		value: the new value
		prevValue: the previous value
	"""
	#debug('\nonTextEditEnd comp:', comp.path, '- new value: ', value, ', prev value: ', prevValue )
	return

2

def onMessageLogged(info):
	"""
	onMessageLogged() is called once everytime a message is logged.

	info - A dict with the following keys
		logItemDict - the referenced COMP of the parameter Comptoexport

	"""
	return

*�����������������"# me is this DAT.
#
# comp is the List Component that holds this panel.
# row is the row number of the cell being updated.
# col is the column number of the cell being updated.
#
# attribs contains the following members:
#
# text				   str			cell contents.
# help				 str			 help text
#
# textColor			r g b a		font color
# textOffsetX		   n			  horizontal text offset
# textOffsetY		   n			  vertical text offset
# textJustify		   m			  m is one of:  JustifyType.TOPLEFT, JustifyType.TOPCENTER,
#													JustifyType.TOPRIGHT, JustifyType.CENTERLEFT,
#													JustifyType.CENTER, JustifyType.CENTERRIGHT,
#													JustifyType.BOTTOMLEFT, JustifyType.BOTTOMCENTER,
#													JustifyType.BOTTOMRIGHT
#
# bgColor			  r g b a		background color
#
# leftBorderInColor	   r g b a		  inside left border color
# rightBorderInColor   r g b a		  inside right border color
# topBorderInColor	   r g b a		  inside top border color
# bottomBorderInColor  r g b a		  inside bottom border color
#
# leftBorderOutColor   r g b a		  outside left border color
# rightBorderOutColor  r g b a		  outside right border colorh
# topBorderOutColor	   r g b a		  outside top border color
# bottomBorderOutColor r g b a		  outside bottom border color
#
# colWidth			 w			  sets column width
# colStetch			True/False	 sets column stretchiness (width is min width)
# rowHeight			h			  sets row height
#
# editable			   bool			  contents are editable
# fontBold			 True/False	 render font bolded
# fontItalic		   True/False	 render font italicized
# fontSizeX			float		  font X size in pixels
# fontSizeX			float		  font Y size in pixels, if not specified, uses X size
# fontFace			 str			  font face, example 'Verdana'
# wordWrap			 True/False	 word wrap
#
# image				TOP			  background TOP operator
#
# select   true when the cell/row/col is currently being selected by the mouse.
# rollover true when the mouse is currently over the cell/row/col.
# radio	true when the cell/row/col was last selected.
# focus	true when the cell/row/col is being edited.
#
# currently not implemented:
#
# type				str			 cell type: 'field' or 'label'
# fieldtype		   str			 field type: 'float' 'string' or  'integer'
# setpos			  True/False x y  set cell absolute when first argument is True
# padding			 l r b t		 cell padding from each edge, expressed in pixels
# margin			  l r b t		 cell margin from neighbouring cells, expressed in pixels
#
# fontpath			path			File location to font. Don't use with 'font'
# fontformat		  str			 font format: 'polygon', 'outline' or 'bitmap'
# fontantialiased	 True/False	  render font antialiased
# fontcharset		 str			 font character set
#
# textjustify		 h v			 left/right/center top/center/bottom
# textoffset		  x y			 text position offset
#

#called when Reset parameter is pulsed, or on load

def onInitCell(comp, row, col, attribs):
	return


def onInitRow(comp, row, attribs):
	return


def onInitCol(comp, col, attribs):
	return


def onInitTable(comp, attribs):
	try:
		listerExt = ext.ListerExt
	except:
		pass
	else:
		listerExt.onInitTable(attribs)

	return

#called during specific events
#
# coords is a named tuple containing the following members:
#   x
#   y
#   u
#   v


def onRollover(comp, row, col, coords, prevrow, prevcol, prevcoords):
	# debug('rollover', row, col)
	ext.ListerExt.onRollover(row, col, coords, prevrow, prevcol,
										prevcoords)
	return


def onSelect(comp, startrow, startcol, startcoords, endrow, endcol, endcoords,
			 start, end):
	# print(me, 'onSelect', comp, startrow, start, end)
	ext.ListerExt.onSelect(startrow, startcol, startcoords, endrow,
										endcol, endcoords, start, end)
	return


def onRadio(comp, row, col, prevrow, prevcol):
	# print(onRadio, comp, row, col)
	return


def onFocus(comp, row, col, prevrow, prevcol):
	ext.ListerExt.onFocus( row, col, prevrow, prevcol)
	return

def onEdit(comp, row, col, val):
	# print("onEdit", row, col, val)
	ext.ListerExt.onEdit( row, col, val)
	return

# return True if interested in this drop event, False otherwise
def onHover(comp, row, col, coords,	prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems):
	if parent.Lister.par.drop.eval() != 'dropno' \
			and parent.Lister.fetch('legacyDragDrop', 0):
		retVal = ext.ListerExt.onDropOrHover(True, comp, row, col,
								coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems)
		return retVal

def onDrop(comp, row, col, coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems):
	if parent.Lister.par.drop.eval() != 'dropno' \
			and parent.Lister.fetch('legacyDragDrop', 0):
		return ext.ListerExt.onDropOrHover(False, comp, row, col,
								coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems)

*�����������������{# me - this DAT.
# 
# dat - the changed DAT
# rows - a list of row indices
# cols - a list of column indices
# cells - the list of cells that have changed content
# prev - the list of previous string contents of the changed cells
# 
# Make sure the corresponding toggle is enabled in the DAT Execute DAT.
# 
# If rows or columns are deleted, sizeChange will be called instead of row/col/cellChange.


def tableChange(dat):
	if dat.numCols != ext.ListerExt.nCols and parent.Lister.par.Autodefinecols.eval():
		ext.ListerExt.setupAutoColDefine(True)

	parent.Lister.Refresh()
	return

def rowChange(dat, rows):
	if 0 in rows and parent.Lister.par.Autodefinecols.eval() and parent.Lister.par.Header.eval():
		run('args[0].setupAutoColDefine(True)',ext.ListerExt,delayFrames=1)
	return

def colChange(dat, cols):
	return

def cellChange(dat, cells, prev):
	return

def sizeChange(dat):
	return

def onDrop(comp, row, col, coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems):
	if parent.Lister.par.drop.eval() != 'dropno' \
			and parent.Lister.fetch('legacyDragDrop', 0):
		return ext.ListerExt.onDropOrHover(False, comp, row, col,
								coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems)

# If rows or columns are deleted, sizeChange will be called instead of row/col/cellChange.


def tableChange(dat):
	if dat.numCols != ext.ListerExt.nCols and parent.Lister.par.Autodefinecols.eval():
		ext.ListerExt.setupAutoColDefine(True)

	parent.Lister.Refresh()
	return

def rowChange(dat, rows):
	if 0 in rows and parent.Lister.par.Autodefinecols.eval() and parent.Lister.par.Header.eval():
		run('args[0].setupAutoColDefine(True)',ext.ListerExt,delayFrames=1)
	return

def colChange(dat, cols):
	return

def cellChange(dat, cells, prev):
	return

def sizeChange(dat):
	return

*�����������������&*"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner components with python. An
extension is accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from any operator
within the extended component. If the extension is promoted via its
Promote Extension parameter, all its attributes with capitalized names
can be accessed externally, e.g. op('yourComp').PromotedFunction().

Help: search "Extensions" in wiki
"""

from TDStoreTools import StorageManager
import TDFunctions as TDF
from typing import List
import threading
from enum import Enum

class ThreadsMonitorExt:
	"""
	ThreadsMonitorExt description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp) -> None:
		"""
		This is the extension for the Threads Monitor. 
		This component extension is not meant to be accessed from the network.
		While its methods are documented, most remain non-promoted.

		The ThreadsMonitor is a small component to be used as a UI top the ThreadManager if in need of a live debugging tool. 
		It's useful to track what a TDThread is doing or how busy the ThreadManager currently is.

		It's also listing all the TDTasks, what's in queue or currently being processed.
		"""
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp

		self.threadManager = self.ownerComp.par.Threadmanager.eval()
		self.logger = self.threadManager.Logger if self.threadManager else None
		self.safeLogger = self.logger.Logger if self.logger and hasattr(self.logger, 'Logger') and self.logger.Logger else None
		self.data = self.ownerComp.op('data')
		self.tasksData = self.ownerComp.op('dataTasksQueue')
		self.logger.Debug('ThreadsMonitor initialized.')

	def onInitTD(self) -> None:
		"""
		When the extension initialized, the worker threads are created and
		multiple attributes get updated with post-init values.
		"""
		self.initData()

	def initData(self) -> None:
		"""
		On extension init, setup existing threads
		"""
		self.data.clear()
		self.tasksData.clear()
		
		if self.threadManager:
			threads = self.threadManager.Threads
			for thread in threads:
				self.addThreadData(thread)
			
			tasks = self.threadManager.Tasks
			for task in tasks:
				self.addTaskData(task)	
			
		return

	def refreshData(self) -> None:
		if self.threadManager:
			self.updateThreadsData()
			self.updateTasksData()

	def getCurrentThreadList(self) -> List[str]:
		"""
		The threads that are currently stored and being displayed in the ThreadsMonitor UI.

		Returns:
			List[str]: The list of thread IDs.
		"""
		if not self.data.numCols:
			return None
		
		return self.data.col(0, val=True)

	def getNewThreads(self, currentThreadsList: List[str], threadManagerThreads: List[str]) -> List[str]:
		"""
		Return a list of new threads, or threads that are missing from the ThreadsMonitor against
		the ThreadManager storage.

		Args:
			currentThreadsList (List[str]): The list of threads currently stored in the Data DAT of the ThreadsMonitor.
			threadManagerThreads (List[str]): The list of threads currently stored in the ThreadManager extension.

		Returns:
			List[str]: The new threads ids, as str.
		"""
		if currentThreadsList and threadManagerThreads:
			new_threads = set(threadManagerThreads) - set(currentThreadsList)
		elif threadManagerThreads:
			new_threads = set(threadManagerThreads)
		else:
			return list()
		
		return list(new_threads)

	def addThreadData(self, tdThread: threading.Thread) -> None:
		"""
		Add a new thread to the Data DAT, 
		after being passed a TDThread object.

		Args:
			tdThread (threading.Thread): The thread to add to the Data DAT.
		"""
		with tdThread.StateLock:
			self.data.appendRow([
				tdThread.ident,
				tdThread.name,
				tdThread.InPool,
				tdThread.TDTask.target.__name__ if tdThread.TDTask else tdThread.target.__name__ if not tdThread.InPool else None,
				tdThread.SuccessHook.__name__ if tdThread.SuccessHook else None,
				tdThread.ExceptHook.__name__ if tdThread.ExceptHook else None,
				tdThread.RefreshHook.__name__ if tdThread.RefreshHook else None,
				['Terminated', 'Alive'][tdThread.is_alive()],
				tdThread.State.name,
				tdThread.Progress
			])
		return

	def updateThreadsData(self) -> None:
		"""
		Update all currently existing threads from the Data DAT.
		It's fetching up to date information about the running threads from the 
		referenced threadManager.
		"""
		storedIDs = self.data.col(0, val=True)
		threadIDs = []
		with self.threadManager.ManagerCondition:
			threadIDs = [str(thread.ident) for thread in self.threadManager.Threads]		

		if storedIDs and threadIDs:
			if set(storedIDs) != set(threadIDs):
				self.initData()
				return
			
		for row in self.data.rows():
			threadID = row[0].val

			tdThread = self.threadManager.GetThreadById(threadID)

			if tdThread:
				with tdThread.StateLock:
					self.data.replaceRow(
						threadID,
						[
							threadID,
							row[1].val,
							row[2].val,
							tdThread.TDTask.target.__name__ if tdThread.TDTask else tdThread.target.__name__ if not tdThread.InPool else None,
							tdThread.SuccessHook.__name__ if tdThread.SuccessHook else None,
							tdThread.ExceptHook.__name__ if tdThread.ExceptHook else None,
							tdThread.RefreshHook.__name__ if tdThread.RefreshHook else None,
							['Terminated', 'Alive'][tdThread.is_alive()],
							tdThread.State.name,
							tdThread.Progress
						]
					)
		
		return

	def removeThreadData(self, threadId: str):
		"""
		Remove thread data from the Data DAT,
		provided a (string) of a Thread ID.

		Args:
			threadId (str): The thread ID to be removed form the Data DAT.
		"""
		self.data.deleteRow(threadId)

	def getCurrentTasksList(self) -> List[str]:
		"""
		The tasks that are currently stored and being displayed in the ThreadsMonitor UI.

		Returns:
			List[str]: The list of tasks IDs.
		"""
		if not self.tasksData.numCols:
			return None
		
		return self.tasksData.col(0, val=True)

	def getNewTasks(self, currentTaskList: List[str], tasksInQueue: List[str]) -> List[str]:
		"""
		Return a list of new tasks, or tasks that are missing from the ThreadsMonitor against
		the ThreadManager storage.

		Args:
			currentTaskList (List[str]): The list of tasks currently stored in the DataTasksQueue DAT of the ThreadsMonitor.
			tasksInQueue (List[str]): The list of tasks currently stored in the ThreadManager extension.

		Returns:
			List[str]: The new tasks IDs, as str.
		"""
		if currentTaskList and tasksInQueue:
			new_tasks = set(tasksInQueue) - set(currentTaskList)
		elif tasksInQueue:
			new_tasks = set(tasksInQueue)
		else:
			return list()
		
		return list(new_tasks)
	
	def addTaskData(self, tdTask: object) -> None:
		"""
		Add a TDTask to the DataTasksQueue DAT of the ThreadsMonitor.

		Args:
			tdTask (object): The TDTask to be added.
		"""
		self.tasksData.appendRow([
			str(id(tdTask)),
			tdTask.target.__name__,
			tdTask.SuccessHook.__name__ if tdTask.SuccessHook else None,
			tdTask.ExceptHook.__name__ if tdTask.ExceptHook else None,
			tdTask.RefreshHook.__name__ if tdTask.RefreshHook else None,
			tdTask.RanBy
		])
		return

	def updateTasksData(self) -> None:
		"""
		Update all currently existing threads from the Data DAT.
		It's fetching up to date information about the running threads from the 
		referenced threadManager.
		"""
		for row in self.tasksData.rows():
			taskID = row[0].val

			tdTask = self.threadManager.GetTaskById(taskID)

			if tdTask:		
				self.tasksData.replaceRow(
					taskID,
					[
						taskID,
						row[1].val,
						row[2].val,
						row[3].val,
						row[4].val,
						tdTask.RanBy
					]
				)
		return

	def removeTaskData(self, taskId: str) -> None:
		"""
		Remove a TDTask from the DataTasksQueue DAT of the ThreadsMonitor.

		Args:
			taskId (str): The TDTask ID to be removed.		
		"""
		self.tasksData.deleteRow(taskId)
		return
	
	"""
	Utilities
	"""
	def Reset(self) -> None:
		"""
		Reset the ThreadsMonitor COMP to a default state.
		"""
		self.initData()

	"""
	Par Events
	"""
	def OnPulseReset(self, par):
		self.Reset()
	
	def OnValueChangeThreadmanager(self, par, prev):
		try:
			self.threadManager = par.eval()
			self.initData()
		except Exception as e:
			op.TDResources.TDAppLogger.Error(f'An error occured when evaluating the ThreadManager parameter: {e}')
		
		return

	"""
	ThreadManager Par Events
	"""	
	def OnThreadManagerRunningthreadsChange(self, par, prev):
		if par.eval() > prev:
			currentThreadsList = self.getCurrentThreadList()
			threadManagerThreads = [str(tdThread.ident) for tdThread in self.threadManager.Threads]
			newThreads = self.getNewThreads(currentThreadsList, threadManagerThreads)

			for threadId in newThreads:
				threadToAdd = self.threadManager.GetThreadById(threadId)
				if threadToAdd:
					self.addThreadData(threadToAdd)

		else:
			currentThreadsList = self.getCurrentThreadList()
			threadManagerThreads = [str(tdThread.ident) for tdThread in self.threadManager.Threads]
			removedThreads = self.getNewThreads(threadManagerThreads,currentThreadsList) # Swap currentThreadsList and threadManagerThreads to get the opposite
			
			for threadId in removedThreads:
				self.removeThreadData(threadId)
	

	def OnThreadManagerTotaltasksChange(self, par, prev):
		if par.eval() > prev:
			currentTasksList = self.getCurrentTasksList()
			tasksInQueue = [str(id(tdTask)) for tdTask in self.threadManager.Tasks]
			newTasks = self.getNewTasks(currentTasksList, tasksInQueue)
			
			for taskId in newTasks:
				taskToAdd = self.threadManager.GetTaskById(taskId)
				if taskToAdd:
					self.addTaskData(taskToAdd)
		
		else:
			currentTasksList = self.getCurrentTasksList()
			tasksInQueue = [str(id(tdTask)) for tdTask in self.threadManager.Tasks]
			removedTasks = self.getNewTasks(tasksInQueue, currentTasksList) # Swap currentTasksList and tasksInQueue to get the opposite
			
			for taskId in removedTasks:
				self.removeTaskData(taskId)

		pass

*�����������������y# me - this DAT
# scriptOp - the OP which is cooking
#
# press 'Setup Parameters' in the OP to call this function to re-create the parameters.
def onSetupParameters(scriptOp):
	return

# called whenever custom pulse parameter is pushed
def onPulse(par):
	return

def onCook(scriptOp):
	try:
		scriptOp.text = setupMenuOptions(scriptOp)
	except:
		scriptOp.text = "name\tlabel"
	#scriptOp.copy(scriptOp.inputs[0])	# no need to call .clear() above when copying
	#scriptOp.insertRow(['color', 'size', 'shape'], 0)
	#scriptOp.appendRow(['red', '3', 'square'])
	#scriptOp[1,0] += '**'

	return

def setupMenuOptions(scriptOp):
	if hasattr(parent.Widget.par, 'Synctoboundmenu'):
		parent.Widget.par.Menulabels.readOnly = \
			parent.Widget.par.Menunames.readOnly = \
									parent.Widget.par.Synctoboundmenu.eval()
	externalMenuTable = scriptOp.inputs[0]
	if externalMenuTable.numCols:
		labels = []
		#['AllRows_FirstCols', 'LabelRow_FirstCols', 'LabelRow_LabeledCols']
		if parent.Widget.par.Tableformat.eval() ==\
												'ExcludeFirstRow_LabeledCols':
			names = [c.val for c in externalMenuTable.col('name')[1:]]
			labelCol = externalMenuTable.col('label')
			if labelCol:
				labels = [c.val for c in labelCol[1:]]
		else:
			names = [c.val for c in externalMenuTable.col(0)]
			labelCol = externalMenuTable.col(1)
			if labelCol:
				labels = [c.val for c in labelCol]
			if parent.Widget.par.Tableformat.eval() == \
												'ExcludeFirstRow_NumberedCols':
				names.pop(0)
				if labels:
					labels.pop(0)
	else:
		names = tdu.split(parent.Widget.par.Menunames)
		labels = tdu.split(parent.Widget.par.Menulabels)
	finalNames = names
	finalLabels = []
	for i, name in enumerate(names):
		if i < len(labels):
			finalLabels.append(labels[i])
		else:
			finalLabels.append(name)
	return 'name\tlabel\n' + \
			'\n'.join([finalNames[i] + '\t' + finalLabels[i]
					   		for i in range(len(finalNames))])

"""
PopMenu callbacks

Callbacks always take a single argument, which is a dictionary
of values relevant to the callback. Print this dictionary to see what is
being passed. The keys explain what each item is.

onSelect: user selects a menu option
onMouseOver: mouse passes over a cell
onMouseDown: left button pressed
onMouseUp: left button released
onClick: left button pressed then released on same cell
onLostFocus: The popmenu focus has been lost

Callbacks generally return a dict with:
	'cell': either cell id or -1 for no cell
	'label': the label in the menu list
	'row': the row from the wired dat input, if applicable
	'details': details provided by object that caused menu to open
"""

text.content:

*�����������������"# me is this DAT.
#
# comp is the List Component that holds this panel.
# row is the row number of the cell being updated.
# col is the column number of the cell being updated.
#
# attribs contains the following members:
#
# text				   str			cell contents.
# help				 str			 help text
#
# textColor			r g b a		font color
# textOffsetX		   n			  horizontal text offset
# textOffsetY		   n			  vertical text offset
# textJustify		   m			  m is one of:  JustifyType.TOPLEFT, JustifyType.TOPCENTER,
#													JustifyType.TOPRIGHT, JustifyType.CENTERLEFT,
#													JustifyType.CENTER, JustifyType.CENTERRIGHT,
#													JustifyType.BOTTOMLEFT, JustifyType.BOTTOMCENTER,
#													JustifyType.BOTTOMRIGHT
#
# bgColor			  r g b a		background color
#
# leftBorderInColor	   r g b a		  inside left border color
# rightBorderInColor   r g b a		  inside right border color
# topBorderInColor	   r g b a		  inside top border color
# bottomBorderInColor  r g b a		  inside bottom border color
#
# leftBorderOutColor   r g b a		  outside left border color
# rightBorderOutColor  r g b a		  outside right border colorh
# topBorderOutColor	   r g b a		  outside top border color
# bottomBorderOutColor r g b a		  outside bottom border color
#
# colWidth			 w			  sets column width
# colStetch			True/False	 sets column stretchiness (width is min width)
# rowHeight			h			  sets row height
#
# editable			   bool			  contents are editable
# fontBold			 True/False	 render font bolded
# fontItalic		   True/False	 render font italicized
# fontSizeX			float		  font X size in pixels
# fontSizeX			float		  font Y size in pixels, if not specified, uses X size
# fontFace			 str			  font face, example 'Verdana'
# wordWrap			 True/False	 word wrap
#
# image				TOP			  background TOP operator
#
# select   true when the cell/row/col is currently being selected by the mouse.
# rollover true when the mouse is currently over the cell/row/col.
# radio	true when the cell/row/col was last selected.
# focus	true when the cell/row/col is being edited.
#
# currently not implemented:
#
# type				str			 cell type: 'field' or 'label'
# fieldtype		   str			 field type: 'float' 'string' or  'integer'
# setpos			  True/False x y  set cell absolute when first argument is True
# padding			 l r b t		 cell padding from each edge, expressed in pixels
# margin			  l r b t		 cell margin from neighbouring cells, expressed in pixels
#
# fontpath			path			File location to font. Don't use with 'font'
# fontformat		  str			 font format: 'polygon', 'outline' or 'bitmap'
# fontantialiased	 True/False	  render font antialiased
# fontcharset		 str			 font character set
#
# textjustify		 h v			 left/right/center top/center/bottom
# textoffset		  x y			 text position offset
#

#called when Reset parameter is pulsed, or on load

def onInitCell(comp, row, col, attribs):
	return


def onInitRow(comp, row, attribs):
	return


def onInitCol(comp, col, attribs):
	return


def onInitTable(comp, attribs):
	try:
		listerExt = ext.ListerExt
	except:
		pass
	else:
		listerExt.onInitTable(attribs)

	return

#called during specific events
#
# coords is a named tuple containing the following members:
#   x
#   y
#   u
#   v


def onRollover(comp, row, col, coords, prevrow, prevcol, prevcoords):
	# debug('rollover', row, col)
	ext.ListerExt.onRollover(row, col, coords, prevrow, prevcol,
										prevcoords)
	return


def onSelect(comp, startrow, startcol, startcoords, endrow, endcol, endcoords,
			 start, end):
	# print(me, 'onSelect', comp, startrow, start, end)
	ext.ListerExt.onSelect(startrow, startcol, startcoords, endrow,
										endcol, endcoords, start, end)
	return


def onRadio(comp, row, col, prevrow, prevcol):
	# print(onRadio, comp, row, col)
	return


def onFocus(comp, row, col, prevrow, prevcol):
	ext.ListerExt.onFocus( row, col, prevrow, prevcol)
	return

def onEdit(comp, row, col, val):
	# print("onEdit", row, col, val)
	ext.ListerExt.onEdit( row, col, val)
	return

# return True if interested in this drop event, False otherwise
def onHover(comp, row, col, coords,	prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems):
	if parent.Lister.par.drop.eval() != 'dropno' \
			and parent.Lister.fetch('legacyDragDrop', 0):
		retVal = ext.ListerExt.onDropOrHover(True, comp, row, col,
								coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems)
		return retVal

def onDrop(comp, row, col, coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems):
	if parent.Lister.par.drop.eval() != 'dropno' \
			and parent.Lister.fetch('legacyDragDrop', 0):
		return ext.ListerExt.onDropOrHover(False, comp, row, col,
								coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems)

*�����������������{# me - this DAT.
# 
# dat - the changed DAT
# rows - a list of row indices
# cols - a list of column indices
# cells - the list of cells that have changed content
# prev - the list of previous string contents of the changed cells
# 
# Make sure the corresponding toggle is enabled in the DAT Execute DAT.
# 
# If rows or columns are deleted, sizeChange will be called instead of row/col/cellChange.


def tableChange(dat):
	if dat.numCols != ext.ListerExt.nCols and parent.Lister.par.Autodefinecols.eval():
		ext.ListerExt.setupAutoColDefine(True)

	parent.Lister.Refresh()
	return

def rowChange(dat, rows):
	if 0 in rows and parent.Lister.par.Autodefinecols.eval() and parent.Lister.par.Header.eval():
		run('args[0].setupAutoColDefine(True)',ext.ListerExt,delayFrames=1)
	return

def colChange(dat, cols):
	return

def cellChange(dat, cells, prev):
	return

def sizeChange(dat):
	return

*�����������������SubWidgetExt = mod.SubWidgetExt.SubWidgetExt
TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions

class ButtonExt(SubWidgetExt):
	"""
	ButtonExt provides all functionality for basic widget button
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.parPrefix = 'Button'
		SubWidgetExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		self.valueIndex = self.ownerComp.digits

		self.types = [
				('Momentary', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.SetValue(1),
							   'onToOff': lambda p: self.SetValue(0)}),
				('Momentary Up', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.SetValue(0),
								  'onToOff': lambda p: self.SetValue(1)}),
				('Toggle Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Toggle()}),
				('Toggle Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Toggle(checkInside=True)}),
				('Toggle Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Toggle()}),
				('Radio Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Radio Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Radio(checkInside=True)}),
				('Radio Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Exclusive Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Exclusive Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Radio(checkInside=True)}),
				('Exclusive Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Commands Only', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.RunOnCommand(),
							   	   'onToOff': lambda p: self.RunOffCommand()}),
				('Commands Only Ignore Value', {
								   'offToOn': lambda p: self.RunOnCommand(),
							   	   'onToOff': lambda p: self.RunOffCommand()})
		]
		self.buttonTypePar = parent.Widget.par.Buttontype
		self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuLabels = [type[0] for type in self.types]
		self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuNames = self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuLabels

	def SetValue(self, value):
		"""
		Sets the value of the widget
		"""
		setattr(self.ownerComp.parent.Widget.par,
										'Value'+str(self.valueIndex), value)

	def GetValue(self):
		"""
		Sets the value of the widget
		"""
		#debug('value change')
		return getattr(self.ownerComp.parent.Widget.par,
										'Value'+str(self.valueIndex))

	def Radio(self, checkInside=False):
		if self.Toggle(checkInside):
			self.updateRadioGroup()

	def Toggle(self, checkInside=False):
		"""
		Sets the value of the button to on when off, and off when on

		:param checkInside: if True, only toggle if the mouse is currently
			inside the ownerComp panel.
		:return: False if skipped from not inside, otherwise True
		"""
		if checkInside and not self.ownerComp.panel.inside:
			return False
		self.SetValue(1-self.GetValue())
		return True

	def RunOnCommand(self):
		"""
		Runs the python command for when the button turns on
		"""
		#debug('command on')
		command = parent.Widget.par.Offtoonscript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
		command = parent.Widget.par.Onvaluechangescript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)

	def RunOffCommand(self):
		"""
		Runs the python command for when the button turns off
		"""
		#debug('command off')
		command = parent.Widget.par.Ontooffscript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
		command = parent.Widget.par.Onvaluechangescript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
	@property
	def GroupButtons(self):
		table = parent.Widget.par.Buttongroupdat.eval()
		if not isinstance(table, DAT):
			return []
		tableParent = table.parent()
		return [tableParent.op(c.val)
							for c in table.col(0) if tableParent.op(c.val)]

	def onPanelEvent(self, event, panelValue=None):
		"""
		Selects correct funtion based on button widget type
		"""
		#debug('panelEvent', event)
		typeIndex = self.buttonTypePar.menuIndex
		btype = self.types[typeIndex][1].get(event)
		#debug(self.types[typeIndex][0])
		if btype:
			#debug('onPanel', event)
			btype(panelValue)

	def onParValueChange(self, par, prev):
		"""
		The Button's value has changed.
		Only runs when necessary (see parexecValue 'Active' parameter).
		"""
		type = self.buttonTypePar.eval()
		if type == 'Commands_Only_Ignore_Value':
			return
		if type.startswith('Radio') or type.startswith('Exclusive'):
			self.updateRadioGroup()
		# if par.eval():
		# 	self.RunOnCommand()
		# else:
		# 	self.RunOffCommand()

	def updateRadioGroup(self):
		"""Turn off other radio buttons

		:return: False if change reverted because trying to turn off Radio,
			otherwise True
		"""
		#debug('radiogroup')
		type = self.buttonTypePar.eval()
		groupButtons = self.GroupButtons
		if type.startswith('Radio') and parent.Widget.par.Value0 == 0 and \
				not any([button.par.Value0 for button in groupButtons]):
			self.SetValue(1)
			return False
		if parent.Widget.par.Value0:
			for button in groupButtons:
				if button != parent.Widget:
					button.par.Value0 = False
		return True

	def onParSettingsChange(self, par, prev):
		if par.name == self.buttonTypePar.name:
			if par.eval().startswith('Momentary'):
				self.onPanelEvent('onToOff')
			elif par.eval().startswith('Radio') \
									or par.eval().startswith('Exclusive'):
				self.updateRadioGroup()
			parent.Widget.par.Buttongroupdat.enable = \
										par.eval().startswith('Radio') \
										or par.eval().startswith('Exclusive')
		else:
			SubWidgetExt.onParSettingsChange(self, par, prev)
